searchDocs.title: Using VluxySF
searchDocs.content: Using VluxySF Here are some quick links to get started using VluxySF Installation guideAPI DocsInitializationFolder setupExamples

searchDocs.title: Wally Configuration‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Once Wally is installed, run wally init on your project directory, and then add VluxySF as a dependency. For example, the following could be a wally.toml file for a project that includes a few of these modules:  [package] name = "your_name/your_project" version = "0.1.0" registry = "https://github.com/UpliftGames/wally-index" realm = "shared" [dependencies] VluxySF = "greenviper126/VluxySF@^1"   To install this dependency, run wally install within your project. Wally will create a Package folder in your directory with the installed dependency.

searchDocs.title: Rojo Configuration‚Äč
searchDocs.content: The Package folder created by Wally should be synced into Roblox Studio through your Rojo configuration. For instance, a Rojo configuration might have the following entry to sync the Packages folder into ReplicatedStorage:  { "name": "vluxysf", "tree": { "$className": "DataModel", "ReplicatedStorage": { "$className": "ReplicatedStorage", "Packages": { "$path": "Packages" } } } }

searchDocs.title: 1. Setting Up the Sound Folder‚Äč
searchDocs.content: First, create a Configuration (commonly named SOUNDS) containing all your sound instances. For details on organizing this folder, see Folder Setup.

searchDocs.title: 2. Server Initialization‚Äč
searchDocs.content: On the server, inject the folder or configuration containing your instanced sounds. This folder should be located in a service like ServerStorage.  local ServerStorage = game:GetService("ServerStorage") local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) -- Reference to the folder/config of the actual sounds you want to use local soundFolder = ServerStorage:FindFirstChild("SOUNDS") -- Initialize the server VluxySF._initServer(soundFolder)

searchDocs.title: 3. Client Initialization‚Äč
searchDocs.content: On the client, simply call the initialization function:  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) -- Initialize the client VluxySF._initClient()

searchDocs.title: Basic Playback (Client/Server)‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Fetch and play a sound instance by name. This works on both client and server.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() -- Wait until schema is loaded local sound = VluxySF.ConstructSoundFromName("MUSIC/LoFi/beat1") -- waits until sound is loaded if sound then sound:Play() end   "Music/LoFi/Beat1" is just an example path in your SOUNDS folder.

searchDocs.title: Manipulating SoundGroup Volumes‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Set the SoundGroup volumes at the start of a session. This can be done on server or client (client is recommended for user settings).  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() local masterVolume = 0.8 local subVolumes = { ["GAME"] = 0.7, ["MUSIC"] = 0.55, ["SFX"] = 0.64, } local function UpdateSoundGroupVolumes() for _, soundGroup in ipairs(VluxySF.GetSoundGroups()) do local rawVolume = subVolumes[soundGroup.Name] or 0.5 local realVolume = rawVolume * masterVolume soundGroup.Volume = realVolume end end UpdateSoundGroupVolumes()   Tip: You can call this function whenever a slider or setting changes to update volumes live.

searchDocs.title: Simple Use‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Create and play a looping sound using the SoundEntity API:  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() local endingMusic = VluxySF.Create("MUSIC/LoFi/beat1"):SetLooped(true):Play()

searchDocs.title: _Preload Startup Example‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Sometimes you need a sound to be loaded on startup or the first time it‚Äôs played. There is no difference in fetching the sound besides the extra child location.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() local endingMusic = VluxySF.Create("SFX/Guns/_Preload/shotgunFired1"):PlayOnce()

searchDocs.title: _Preload SoundEntity Example‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Sometimes you don‚Äôt know if something needs to be preloaded until the game starts. In that case, you can preload your fetched sound manually:  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() -- Preload as soon as possible local endingMusic = VluxySF.Create("MUSIC/LoFi/beat1"):Preload() -- yields -- do stuff here endingMusic:Play() -- this sound plays first try

searchDocs.title: Yielding on a Separate Thread‚Äč
searchDocs.content: If you want to deal with sounds on a separate thread, you can use Fork. To clean up any forks, use RemoveForks.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.VluxySF) VluxySF.WaitForSchema() local endingMusic = VluxySF.Create("MUSIC/LoFi/beat1"):Fork(function(self) self:Sleep(10):Play() -- sleep method waits for 10 seconds end)   This waits for 10 seconds and then plays the sound on a separate thread.

searchDocs.title: SoundEffectDefinition‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundEffectDefinition = Dictionary<any> Defines the properties of a sound effect as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: SoundEffectDefinitions‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundEffectDefinitions = Dictionary<SoundEffectDefinition> Defines the sound effects of a sound as a dictionary of class names to their corresponding properties.

searchDocs.title: SoundProperties‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundProperties = Dictionary<any> Defines the properties of a sound as a dictionary of property names and values.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinition‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundDefinition { Properties:¬†SoundProperties-- The properties of the sound itself, such as SoundId, Volume, etc. Effects:¬†SoundEffectDefinitions-- The sound effects of the sound, organized by class name. } Defines the properties and sound effects of a sound.

searchDocs.title: SoundDefinitions‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundDefinitions = Dictionary<SoundDefinition> Defines the sound definitions of the system as a dictionary of sound names to their corresponding sound data.

searchDocs.title: SoundGroups‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundGroups = Dictionary<SoundGroup > Defines the type of a sound group instance.

searchDocs.title: SoundSchema‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> interface SoundSchema { UnattachedSounds:¬†Configuration -- A folder for storing unattached sounds. SoundGroupContainer:¬†Configuration -- A folder for storing sound groups. SoundGroups:¬†SoundGroups-- A dictionary of sound group instances, organized by name. SoundDefinitions:¬†SoundDefinitions-- A dictionary of sound definitions, organized by sound name. PreloadIds:¬†{string}-- A list of sound asset IDs to preload. } Defines the overall schema for the sound system, containing all necessary data for sound creation and management.

searchDocs.title: IsInitialized‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.IsInitialized() ‚Üí¬†boolean-- Whether the sound schema has been initialized. Checks if the sound schema has been initialized. if Sounds.IsInitialized() then print("Sound schema is ready to use!") else print("Sound schema is not initialized yet. Please wait!") end

searchDocs.title: WaitForSchema‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> VluxySF.WaitForSchema() ‚Üí¬†boolean-- Whether the sound schema is ready for use. Waits until the sound schema is initialized and ready for use. If you know that your code will execute after the schema is loaded then this is not needed. print("Waiting for sound system to initialize...") Sounds.WaitForSchema() print("Sound system is now initialized and ready to use!") --now you can use functions and methods that require the schema!

searchDocs.title: GetSoundDefinitions‚Äč
searchDocs.content: RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.GetSoundDefinitions() ‚Üí¬†SoundDefinitions-- All converted sound data Returns all serialized sound data. local definitions = Sounds.GetSoundDefinitions() for location, data in pairs(definitions) do print("Sound location:", location) print("Sound properties:", data.Properties) print("Sound effects:", data.Effects) end

searchDocs.title: GetSoundLocations‚Äč
searchDocs.content: RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.GetSoundLocations() ‚Üí¬†{string}-- All registered sound locations Returns all sound locations. local locations = Sounds.GetSoundLocations() for _, location in ipairs(locations) do print("Sound location:", location) end

searchDocs.title: GetSoundGroup‚Äč
searchDocs.content: RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.GetSoundGroup( name:¬†string-- Name of the sound group ) ‚Üí¬†SoundGroup? -- Matching sound group, if found Finds a sound group by name. local groupName = "MY_SOUND_GROUP" local soundGroup = Sounds.GetSoundGroup(groupName) if soundGroup then print("Found sound group:", groupName) else print("Sound group not found:", groupName) end

searchDocs.title: GetSoundGroups‚Äč
searchDocs.content: RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.GetSoundGroups() ‚Üí¬†SoundGroups-- All sound groups Returns all sound groups. local soundGroups = Sounds.GetSoundGroups() for name, group in pairs(soundGroups) do print("Sound group name:", name) print("Sound group instance:", group) end

searchDocs.title: GetSoundGroupNames‚Äč
searchDocs.content: RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.GetSoundGroupNames() ‚Üí¬†{string}-- All registered sound group names Returns all sound group names. local groupNames = Sounds.GetSoundGroupNames() print("Registered sound groups:", table.concat(groupNames, ", "))

searchDocs.title: Create‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundEntity RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.Create( sound:¬†string¬†|¬†SoundDefinition-- The sound location or a sound definition to attach to. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity-- Constructed class A constructor for manipulating your sounds. local soundLocation = "MY_SOUND/SoundFolder/mySound" local soundEntity = Sounds.Create(soundLocation) soundEntity:Play():Await(30):Reset():FadeIn(4)

searchDocs.title: ConstructSoundFromDefinition‚Äč
searchDocs.content: RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.ConstructSoundFromDefinition( soundDefinition:¬†SoundDefinition-- The sound definition to construct from. ) ‚Üí¬†Sound -- Constructed sound instance based on the provided definition. Constructs a sound instance from a given sound definition, applying all specified properties and effects. local soundDefinition: SoundDefinition = { Properties = { SoundId = "rbxassetid://123456789", Volume = 0.3, }, Effects = { PitchShift = { ClassName = "PitchShiftSoundEffect", Octave = 0.8, }, }, } local soundInstance = Sounds.ConstructSoundFromDefinition(soundDefinition) soundInstance:Play()

searchDocs.title: CreateSoundDefinitionFromSound‚Äč
searchDocs.content: RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.CreateSoundDefinitionFromSound( sound:¬†Sound -- The sound instance to convert into a sound definition. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundDefinition-- A sound definition representing the provided sound instance. Creates a sound definition from a Roblox Sound instance by serializing its properties and valid child effects. This does not get added to the definitions list. local soundInstance: Sound = --[[ reference to an existing Sound instance ]] local soundDefinition = Sounds.CreateSoundDefinitionFromSound(soundInstance) print("Sound properties:", soundDefinition.Properties) print("Sound effects:", soundDefinition.Effects)

searchDocs.title: ConstructSoundFromName‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields RequiresSchema </> VluxySF.ConstructSoundFromName( name:¬†string-- The name of the sound definition to construct from. ) ‚Üí¬†Sound? -- Constructed sound instance if the definition is found, otherwise nil. Constructs a sound instance from a sound definition identified by its name. local soundName = "MY_SOUND/SoundFolder/mySound" local soundInstance = Sounds.ConstructSoundFromName(soundName) if soundInstance then soundInstance:Play() end

searchDocs.title: GetEstimatedPlayLength‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.GetEstimatedPlayLength( sound:¬†Sound -- The sound instance to analyze. ) ‚Üí¬†number-- The estimated play length of the sound, including any variations in playback speed. Calculates the estimated play length of a sound instance by considering its SoundId and any attached sound effects that may influence its duration. local soundInstance: Sound = --[[ reference to an existing Sound instance ]] local estimatedLength = Sounds.GetEstimatedPlayLength(soundInstance) print("Estimated play length:", estimatedLength, "seconds")

searchDocs.title: Variance‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VluxySF.Variance( min:¬†number?,-- Minimum multiplier for variance (default: 0.95) max:¬†number?-- Maximum multiplier for variance (default: 1.05) ) ‚Üí¬†()¬†‚Üí¬†number-- variance result Calculates a random variance value within the specified range. local varianceFunction = Sounds.Variance(0.9, 1.1) local randomVariance1 = varianceFunction() print("Random variance value:", randomVariance) local randomVariance2 = varianceFunction() print("Another random variance value:", randomVariance2)

searchDocs.title: The Basics: Folder Structure‚Äč
searchDocs.content: VluxySF uses your folder structure to automatically group and identify sounds. You do not need to manually assign SoundGroups or create them.

searchDocs.title: Why Structure Matters‚Äč
searchDocs.content: A well-organized sound folder means:  Instantly accessible sounds by stringAutomatic grouping for volume/effect controlEasy preloading for lag-free playbackCleaner, more maintainable projects    Legend:  ‚öôÔłŹ = Configuration Instance ūüďĀ = Folder Instance ūüĒä = Sound Instance   Naming Conventions:  UPPER_CASE for SoundGroupsPascalCase for FolderscamelCase for SoundsAvoid spaces and special characters for best compatibility.Sound locations are used as keys for programmatic access (e.g., Sounds.Create("MUSIC/Explosions/explosion1")).    Example:  SOUNDS‚öôÔłŹ ‚Üź Base file ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ MUSIC‚öôÔłŹ ‚Üź Name of SoundGroup ‚Ēā ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ mainThemeūüĒä ‚Üź Grouped to MUSIC SoundGroup ‚Ēā ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ battleūüĒä ‚Üź Grouped to MUSIC SoundGroup ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ SFX‚öôÔłŹ ‚Üź Name of SoundGroup ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ clickūüĒä ‚Üź Grouped to SFX SoundGroup ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ explosionūüĒä ‚Üź Grouped to SFX SoundGroup   Music and SFX become SoundGroups, and their children are grouped accordingly.  Tip: Once SoundGroupConfigurations are defined (MUSIC, SFX, etc.), organization within them is your choice. Tip: It is recommended that you only use Folders and Sound Instances while organizing within a defined SoundGroupConfigurations. The only exception is _Preload (which should be a Configuration) and adding SoundEffects to a Sound Instance.

searchDocs.title: Organizing with Subfolders‚Äč
searchDocs.content: You can use additional folders (ūüďĀ) inside SoundGroups to keep your sounds organized. Doing so will effect the location of the sound when fetching.  Example:  SOUNDS‚öôÔłŹ ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ SFX‚öôÔłŹ ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ UIūüďĀ ‚Ēā ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ clickūüĒä ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ GameūüďĀ ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ explosionūüĒä   Tip: Plan your folder organization early. Changing a sound‚Äôs location means you must update its path in code too. Tip: You can have the same Sound Instance in different locations. But if they are both direct children of a folder, they must have different names or only one will exist in the SoundDefinitions.

searchDocs.title: Preloading Sounds Automatically‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Want certain sounds to be ready instantly? Any folder named _Preload (with the underscore) will have all its sounds (and subfolders) preloaded automatically on the client.  How to use:  SOUNDS‚öôÔłŹ ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ MUSIC‚öôÔłŹ ‚Ēā ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ mainThemeūüĒä ‚Ēā ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ battleūüĒä ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ _Preload‚öôÔłŹ <-- all children will be preloaded ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ importantSound1ūüĒä ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ importantSound2ūüĒä ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ SFX‚öôÔłŹ ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ clickūüĒä ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ explosionūüĒä ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ _Preload‚öôÔłŹ <-- all children will be preloaded ‚Ēā ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ importantSound1ūüĒä ‚Ēā ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ importantSound2ūüĒä ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ ExplosionsūüďĀ ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ unimportantSound1ūüĒä ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ unimportantSound2ūüĒä ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ _Preload‚öôÔłŹ <-- all children will be preloaded ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ importantSound1ūüĒä ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ importantSound2ūüĒä   Note: You can have multiple _Preload folders in different SoundGroups if needed. Note: This works similarly to Unity's Resources Folder.

searchDocs.title: Preload Timeout‚Äč
searchDocs.content: You can set an optional timeout (in seconds) for preloading. If preloading takes too long, it will continue in the background. Default is 5 seconds.  local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VluxySF = require(ReplicatedStorage.Packages.VluxySF) local preloadTimeout = 10 VluxySF._initClient(preloadTimeout)

searchDocs.title: Configuring Sound Instances‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Each Sound instance can be customized with any Roblox sound properties and child sound effects:  Properties: Set properties like SoundId, Volume, PlaybackSpeed, etc., directly on the Sound instance.Effects: Add child instances such as EqualizerSoundEffect, ReverbSoundEffect, etc, to the Sound for automatic serialization and reconstruction.  Adding any children that are not Roblox SoundEffects to a Sound in the SOUNDS config will not be serialized.

searchDocs.title: End Result‚Äč
searchDocs.content: A SOUNDS Config should look something like this when you‚Äôre done in Roblox Studio:

searchDocs.title: PreloadCallback‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type PreloadCallback = ( assetId:¬†string, assetFetchStatus:¬†Enum.AssetFetchStatus )¬†‚Üí¬†() Callback function that PreLoadAsync uses.

searchDocs.title: EventCallback‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type EventCallback = ( v1:¬†any, v2:¬†any )¬†‚Üí¬†() Generic callback function for events. The parameters passed to the callback will depend on the specific event being listened to.

searchDocs.title: InstanceEventTypes‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type InstanceEventTypes = "Changed"¬†|¬†"AncestryChanged"¬†|¬†"AttributeChanged"¬†|¬†"ChildAdded"¬†|¬†"ChildRemoved"¬†|¬†"DescendantAdded"¬†|¬†"DescendantRemoving"¬†|¬†"Destroying"¬†|¬†"StyledPropertiesChanged" List of instance event types.

searchDocs.title: SoundEventTypes‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type SoundEventTypes = "DidLoop"¬†|¬†"Ended"¬†|¬†"Loaded"¬†|¬†"Paused"¬†|¬†"Played"¬†|¬†"Resumed"¬†|¬†"Stopped" List of sound event types.

searchDocs.title: new‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity.new( sound:¬†string¬†|¬†SoundDefinition-- The name of the sound definition to use or a full sound definition table. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity-- A new instance of SoundEntity initialized with the specified sound. Constructor --by name local soundLocation = "SOUND_GROUP/FileLocation/soundInstance" local soundEntity1 = SoundEntity.new(soundLocation) --by definition local soundDefinition = { Properties = { Name = "MySound", SoundId = "rbxassetid://123456789", SoundGroup = "GAME" Volume = 0.5, }, } local soundEntity2 = SoundEntity.new(soundDefinition)

searchDocs.title: GetCurrentPlayLength‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:GetCurrentPlayLength() ‚Üí¬†number-- The estimated play length of the sound in seconds. Calculates the estimated play length of the sound based on its properties, including any active playback speed modifications. This can be useful for timing events or effects that should occur when the sound finishes playing. soundEntity:Play():Sleep(4) local playLength = soundEntity:GetCurrentPlayLength()--total length minus 4 sec

searchDocs.title: GetRawSound‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:GetRawSound() ‚Üí¬†Sound -- The raw Sound instance created by this SoundEntity. Provides access to the underlying Sound instance for advanced manipulation or retrieval of properties. local soundInstance = soundEntity:GetRawSound() soundInstance.Volume = 0.5 soundInstance.Parent = workspace

searchDocs.title: ResetToDefinition‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:ResetToDefinition() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Resets the sound instance to match the original sound definition. soundEntity:ResetToDefinition()

searchDocs.title: Fork‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Fork( func:¬†function(self:¬†SoundEntity)-- The function to run in a new thread, which receives the SoundEntity instance as an argument. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Starts a new thread to run the provided function, allowing you to perform concurrent operations on the sound while it is playing. soundEntity:Fork(function(self) self:Sleep(5):Play() end) --no yield

searchDocs.title: RemoveForks‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:RemoveForks() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Cleans up all active forks started with the Fork method, effectively stopping any concurrent operations that were running on the sound. soundEntity:RemoveForks()

searchDocs.title: Preload‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> SoundEntity:Preload( callback:¬†PreloadCallback?-- Optional callback to handle the sound successfully preloading or failing to preload. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Preloads the sound's audio asset to reduce latency when it is first played. This applies to all future sounds with the same assetId. Doing this increases memory usage on the client and is not reversible. Only use if needed! soundEntity:Preload()

searchDocs.title: Sleep‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> SoundEntity:Sleep( time:¬†number-- The amount of time in seconds to wait. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Waits for the specified amount of time before resuming. This can be used to create delays in sound sequences or effects. soundEntity:Sleep(2):Stop() --waits 2 seconds before stopping

searchDocs.title: RunCustom‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> SoundEntity:RunCustom( func:¬†function(self:¬†SoundEntity)-- The custom function to run. ) ‚Üí¬†() Runs a custom function of your choice if code needs to run in a certain sequence. This is similar to Fork but does not run on a separate thread. soundEntity:RunCustom(function(self) --custom logic can go here end):Play() --this will run after RunCustom

searchDocs.title: Await‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> SoundEntity:Await( timeout:¬†number?-- Optional timeout in seconds to wait before automatically cleaning up the sound. If not provided, it will wait indefinitely until the sound finishes playing. ) ‚Üí¬†() Waits for the sound to finish playing or for the specified timeout to elapse before cleaning up the sound instance. soundEntity:Play():Await(10) --waits for sound to finish or timeout

searchDocs.title: PlayWhenLoaded‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> SoundEntity:PlayWhenLoaded() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Plays the sound when the sound on the instance is fully loaded. soundEntity:PlayWhenLoaded()

searchDocs.title: PlayOnce‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:PlayOnce() ‚Üí¬†() Plays the sound and automatically cleans it up after it finishes. This is useful for one-off sound effects that don't need to be reused. soundEntity:PlayOnce()

searchDocs.title: Reset‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Reset() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Stops and Replays the sound from the beginning. This is useful for quickly restarting a sound without having to create a new SoundEntity instance. soundEntity:Reset()

searchDocs.title: Clone‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Clone() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity-- A new SoundEntity instance with the same properties and effects as the original. Creates a clone of the SoundEntity, allowing you to create multiple instances of the same sound definition without modifying the original. local newSoundEntity1 = soundEntity:Clone() local newSoundEntity2 = soundEntity:Clone() soundEntity:Cleanup() print(newSoundEntity1) --soundEntity print(newSoundEntity2) --soundEntity

searchDocs.title: Extend‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Extend() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity-- A new SoundEntity instance with the same properties and effects as the original. Makes a clone that is connected to the original SoundEntity. If the original is cleaned up so is this instance. local extendedSoundEntity = soundEntity:Extend() soundEntity:Cleanup() print(extendedSoundEntity) --nil

searchDocs.title: Cleanup‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Cleanup() ‚Üí¬†() Cleans up the Instance. soundEntity:Cleanup()

searchDocs.title: Destroy‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> SoundEntity:Destroy() ‚Üí¬†() Alias for Cleanup. Cleans up the Instance. soundEntity:Destroy()

searchDocs.title: FadeToRaw‚Äč
searchDocs.content: VolumeFade </> SoundEntity:FadeToRaw( volume:¬†number,-- The target volume to fade to. tweenInfo:¬†TweenInfo -- The TweenInfo object defining the duration and easing style of the fade. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Fades the sound's volume to the specified level over time using a tween. local tweenInfo = TweenInfo.new(2, Enum.EasingStyle.Quad, Enum.EasingDirection.Out) soundEntity:FadeToRaw(0.5, tweenInfo)

searchDocs.title: FadeTo‚Äč
searchDocs.content: VolumeFade </> SoundEntity:FadeTo( volume:¬†number,-- The target volume to fade to. time:¬†number?,-- The duration of the fade in seconds. Defaults to 1 second if not provided. style:¬†Enum.EasingStyle?,-- Optional easing style for the fade. Defaults to Sine if not provided. direction:¬†Enum.EasingDirection?-- Optional easing direction for the fade. Defaults to Out if not provided. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Similar to FadeToRaw but does not need tweenInfo to be passed in. soundEntity:Play():Sleep(4) soundEntity:FadeTo(0.5, 2, Enum.EasingStyle.Quad, Enum.EasingDirection.Out)

searchDocs.title: FadeIn‚Äč
searchDocs.content: VolumeFade </> SoundEntity:FadeIn( time:¬†number,-- The duration of the fade in seconds. style:¬†Enum.EasingStyle?-- Optional easing style for the fade. Defaults to Sine. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Fades the sound's volume up from 0 to its defined volume over the specified duration using a tween. soundEntity:Play():FadeIn(2)

searchDocs.title: FadeOut‚Äč
searchDocs.content: VolumeFade </> SoundEntity:FadeOut( time:¬†number,-- The duration of the fade out in seconds. style:¬†Enum.EasingStyle?-- Optional easing style for the fade out. Defaults to Sine. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Fades the sound's volume down to 0 over the specified duration using a tween. soundEntity:Play():RunCustom(function(self) self:Sleep(self:GetCurrentPlayLength() - 2) self:FadeOut(2) end)

searchDocs.title: FadeStop‚Äč
searchDocs.content: VolumeFade </> SoundEntity:FadeStop() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Stops any active volume tween and retains the current volume level. soundEntity:FadeStop()

searchDocs.title: Play‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundMethod </> SoundEntity:Play() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Plays the sound. soundEntity:Play()

searchDocs.title: Pause‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundMethod </> SoundEntity:Pause() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Pauses the sound. soundEntity:Play():Sleep(2):Pause()

searchDocs.title: Resume‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundMethod </> SoundEntity:Resume() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Resumes the sound if it was paused. soundEntity:Play():Sleep(2):Pause():Sleep(2):Resume()

searchDocs.title: Stop‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundMethod </> SoundEntity:Stop() ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Stops the sound. soundEntity:Play():Sleep(2):Stop()

searchDocs.title: Listen‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundEvent </> SoundEntity:Listen( eventType:¬†SoundEventTypes¬†|¬†InstanceEventTypes,-- The type of event to listen to. func:¬†EventCallback-- The callback function to execute when the event occurs. ) ‚Üí¬†Trove  Listens to a specific event on the Sound instance and executes the provided callback function when the event occurs. EventType Description DidLoop (soundId: string, numOfTimesLooped: number): RBXScriptSignal Ended (soundId: string): RBXScriptSignal Loaded (soundId: string): RBXScriptSignal Paused (soundId: string): RBXScriptSignal Played (soundId: string): RBXScriptSignal Resumed (soundId: string): RBXScriptSignal Stopped (soundId: string): RBXScriptSignal Changed (property: string): RBXScriptSignal AncestryChanged (child: Instance, parent: Instance): RBXScriptSignal AttributeChanged (attribute: string): RBXScriptSignal ChildAdded (child: Instance): RBXScriptSignal ChildRemoved (child: Instance): RBXScriptSignal DescendantAdded (descendant: Instance): RBXScriptSignal DescendantRemoving (descendant: Instance): RBXScriptSignal Destroying (): RBXScriptSignal StyledPropertiesChanged (): RBXScriptSignal soundEntity:Listen("Ended", function() print("The sound has finished playing!") end)

searchDocs.title: SetParent‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetParent( parent:¬†Instance -- The new parent for the sound. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the parent property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetParent(workspace)

searchDocs.title: SetLooped‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetLooped( looped:¬†boolean-- Whether the sound should loop or not. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the Looped property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetLooped(true):Play() --plays forever

searchDocs.title: SetArchivable‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetArchivable( archivable:¬†boolean-- Whether the sound should be archivable. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the Archivable property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetArchivable(true)

searchDocs.title: SetPlayOnRemove‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetPlayOnRemove( playOnRemove:¬†boolean-- Whether the sound should play when removed. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the PlayOnRemove property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetPlayOnRemove(true):Destroy() --plays in place

searchDocs.title: SetLoopRegion‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetLoopRegion( loopRegion:¬†NumberRange -- The loop region range in seconds. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the LoopRegion property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetPlaybackRegionsEnabled(true):SetLoopRegion(NumberRange.new(1, 3)):SetLooped(true):Play() --loops between 1 and 3 seconds

searchDocs.title: SetPlaybackRegion‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetPlaybackRegion( region:¬†NumberRange -- The playback region range in seconds. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the PlaybackRegion property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetPlaybackRegionsEnabled(true):SetPlaybackRegion(NumberRange.new(2, 5)):Play() --plays between 2 and 5 seconds

searchDocs.title: SetPlaybackRegionsEnabled‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetPlaybackRegionsEnabled( enabled:¬†boolean-- Whether playback regions should be enabled. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the PlaybackRegionsEnabled property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetPlaybackRegionsEnabled(true)

searchDocs.title: SetPlaybackSpeed‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetPlaybackSpeed( speed:¬†number-- The playback speed multiplier. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the PlaybackSpeed property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetPlaybackSpeed(1.5):Play() --plays 50% faster

searchDocs.title: SetRollOffMaxDistance‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetRollOffMaxDistance( maxDistance:¬†number-- The maximum distance in studs. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the RollOffMaxDistance property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetRollOffMaxDistance(100)

searchDocs.title: SetRollOffMinDistance‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetRollOffMinDistance( minDistance:¬†number-- The minimum distance in studs. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the RollOffMinDistance property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetRollOffMinDistance(10)

searchDocs.title: SetRollOffMode‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetRollOffMode( rollOffMode:¬†Enum.RollOffMode-- The roll off mode to use. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the RollOffMode property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetRollOffMode(Enum.RollOffMode.Linear)

searchDocs.title: SetSoundGroup‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetSoundGroup( soundGroup:¬†SoundGroup -- The sound group to assign. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the SoundGroup property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetSoundGroup("MUSIC")

searchDocs.title: SetTimePosition‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetTimePosition( timePosition:¬†number-- The time position in seconds. ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the TimePosition property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetTimePosition(10):Play() --plays from 10 seconds in

searchDocs.title: SetVolume‚Äč
searchDocs.content: SoundProperty </> SoundEntity:SetVolume( volume:¬†number-- The volume level (0-10). ) ‚Üí¬†SoundEntity Sets the Volume property of the underlying Sound instance. soundEntity:SetVolume(5)
